description: {"description": "Leaf exhibits a generally ovate shape with serrated margins. Surface texture appears slightly pubescent or covered in fine hairs. Visible venation is reticulate."} {"description": "Presence of a large, irregular necrotic lesion concentrated at the distal portion of the leaf. The lesion exhibits a dark brown to black coloration and appears dry and brittle. Surrounding the necrotic area, there is a halo of chlorosis. A white, powdery growth is visible on both healthy and affected leaf tissue, suggesting a fungal or oomycete etiology."} Lesions are localized to the distal portion of the leaf, with the powdery growth distributed more broadly across the leaf surface. The pattern suggests a potential for systemic spread or spore dispersal from the initial lesion.
